title: Censurados
description: The foundational manifesto of Cuban conscious rap in the duo's voice.
impact: Censurados marked a before and after: uncompromising social critique, metaphors that cut through complacency, and a language millions of young people adopted as a mirror. Here the idea was born that rap could be protest poetry and pride at once.

title: Poesía Esposada
description: Poetry and protest intertwined at the duo's maturity.
impact: Poesía Esposada raised the underground to urban literature. The duo showed tenderness and rage can live in the same verse, and that freedom of thought is also a theme of love, neighborhood, and collective memory.

title: El Atropello
description: The record that consolidated the duo's independent thought.
impact: El Atropello rang loud across a generation: messages of conscience, youth, and dignity that transcended the island. It is the most powerful creative closing of the joint era — a record still heard as a call not to surrender the free word.
